$note: Captured snapshot of the COMPARED forecast/intelligence product for the azimuth benchmark (synthesis/benchmark.py). Quoted by product + capture date; NOT surfaced as an azimuth channel and NOT an L1 source. Refresh with pull_benchmark_foils.py.

foil.title: Inflation and rates pressure from Cuba infrastructure fragility state
foil.scenario_short: Cuba infrastructure fragility state is feeding inflation and rates pressure through energy supply shock, with 41% pressure and 49% state confirmation. For now, the base case stays near 61% over the 30d. Actors absorb...
foil.perspective_strategic: Cuba infrastructure fragility state is feeding inflation and rates pressure through energy supply shock, with 41% pressure and 49% state confirmation is setting the strategic baseline, and the current 59% probability implies a live but not settled risk path.
foil.perspective_contrarian: No directly matched headlines are currently attached to Cuba. If that restraint persists, the forecast can move below the current 59% baseline. Actors prioritize containment and the system drifts toward stabilization unless new hard signals emerge.
foil.actor_lens: Commodity traders: Price whether stress in Cuba becomes durable over the 30d. Rebalance positions if the probability path moves away from 59%.
foil.top_signal: Cuba infrastructure fragility state is feeding inflation and rates pressure through energy supply shock, with 41% pressure and 49% state confirmation

foil.title: Inflation and rates pressure from Black Sea maritime disruption state
foil.scenario_short: Black Sea maritime disruption state, with 41% pressure and 72% state confirmation, will feed inflation and rates pressure through shipping cost shock over the next 30 days, supported by 8 urgent critical signals.
foil.perspective_strategic: The 41% pressure and 72% state confirmation indicate a moderate but active transmission path through shipping cost shock, with commodity traders and policy officials likely to rebalance if the 59% probability shifts.
foil.perspective_contrarian: The 75% confidence level and absence of Black Sea-specific headlines (only Strait of Hormuz mentions) suggest that reversal to 51% is possible if no new matched reporting on Black Sea emerges, allowing stabilization.
foil.actor_lens: Commodity traders: Price whether stress in Black Sea becomes durable over the 30d. Rebalance positions if the probability path moves away from 59%.
foil.top_signal: Black Sea maritime disruption state is feeding inflation and rates pressure through shipping cost shock, with 41% pressure and 72% state confirmation

seismic-infrastructure.absence_reason: No product publishes a seismic forecast — earthquakes are not predictable, so there is no forecast/intel alternative to azimuth's observed USGS record. This is the honest inverse: for an observed natural-hazard fact, the sourced record IS the only trustworthy source.
